business.headline: Maersk places fresh order for 1,000 India-made shipping containersThe announcement came as Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Sarbananda Sonowal, unveiled the first export-import (EXIM) shipping container manufactured in India for the company at the Maersk-CONCOR Inland Container Depot in UP's Dadri.

E20 even powers F1 cars: Auto experts dismiss concerns, back govt amid backlashIndustry experts defended the rollout of E20 fuel, saying extensive testing found no concerns in compatible vehicles. They said the ethanol push is tied to lower emissions, defined standards and reduced oil dependence.

Carlsberg eyes India stock market listing; confidential IPO filing submittedCarlsberg said it has confidentially filed for an IPO of its Indian business. The proposed listing would allow a partial stake sale, subject to approvals and market conditions.

ITR 2026: Do you need to file two income tax returns? Tax department explainsThe new Income-tax Act has changed the way taxpayers talk about return filing, replacing "Assessment Year" with "Tax Year". But does that mean you'll have to file two ITRs? Here's what the tax department says.

Sensex, Nifty end higher on IT rally; HCLTech surges nearly 6%The BSE Sensex rose 261.79 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 77,763.91, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 95.15 points, or 0.39%, to close at 24,270.85.

IRDAI is finally going after the real reason behind insurance mis-sellingIRDAI is discussing a shift from upfront commissions to staggered payouts with the insurance industry. The proposed changes target mis-selling incentives and could tighten disclosure across distributors.

New EPFO Scheme: Will your employer contribute only Rs 1,800 to PF?The new EPFO Scheme, 2026 has raised an important question among salaried employees: will employers now limit their PF contribution to just Rs 1,800 a month? Here's what the new rules actually say.

Over Rs 9,300 crore lies unclaimed in 31 lakh EPF accounts, enough to build 3 IITsAn RTI response revealed that Rs 9,330 crore remains unclaimed in 30.91 lakh inoperative EPF accounts across India. The disclosure comes as the new EPF Scheme, 2026 takes effect, while details on Aadhaar-linked and high-value accounts remain undisclosed.

Planning to buy a plot in Noida? Read this Noida Authority warning firstThe Noida Authority has warned people against buying or selling land under specified khasra numbers in three villages. It said the parcels are acquired or notified land where illegal construction could bring financial and legal trouble.

Missed your EMI by a day? Here's how it can affect your credit scoreDid you miss your EMI by a day and instantly worry about your CIBIL score? Before you panic, here's what actually happens behind the scenes.

AI mega deal: Why HCLTech shares jump nearly 6% today?At around 11 am, HCLTech was trading nearly 6% higher. The rally also helped lift the broader Nifty IT index for the second straight session, even as AI-heavy markets in South Korea and Taiwan witnessed a sharp selloff.

Air India cuts fuel surcharge on North America, Europe flights as oil easesAir India has reduced temporary fuel surcharges on select long-haul routes from July 1. The move follows easing oil prices after the West Asia conflict had pushed up operating costs.
Sensex opens 400 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; HCLTech up 4%The BSE Sensex rose 507 points to 78,009.37, while the NSE Nifty50 gained nearly 170 points to trade at 24,346.25 in early trading.

Is the EPFO portal working today? Here's what PF subscribers should knowEPFO has said member and employer services are expected to resume from July 3 after another maintenance extension. The prolonged upgrade has delayed PF claims, passbook access and compliance tasks for nearly a week.

Zero tax in Form 16? You may still have to file an ITR. Here's whyEven if your employer has deducted no income tax because you qualify for the rebate under Section 87A, you may still be legally required to file an ITR in several situations.

EPF Scheme 2026: What employees and employers need to knowWith around eight crore active EPFO subscribers, the government hopes the updated scheme will make provident fund management simpler, more efficient and better suited to a digital-first workplace.

Will petrol, diesel prices be cut? Hardeep Puri says here's when it's possiblePuri explained that although international crude oil prices have fallen in recent weeks, oil companies are still refining crude that was purchased when prices were much higher during the West Asia conflict.

Centre eyes LIC, PSU stake sales to cushion strain from oil crisis: ReportThe Centre is speeding up stake sales in LIC, Hindustan Zinc and other state-run firms. The move is aimed at creating fiscal room as oil price volatility pressures government finances.

Sensex jumps 579 points as IT stocks shine; Nifty closes above 24,175The BSE Sensex climbed 579.48 points, or 0.75%, to close at 77,502.12, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 169.85 points, or 0.71%, to settle at 24,175.70.

AI rally cracks: Why Asian markets are crashing while Indian IT stocks rallyAsian chip stocks slumped after reports stoked fears of excess AI computing capacity. Indian IT shares, more tied to software spending, rose as investors weighed a different AI demand story.

entertainment.headline: Diljit Dosanjh reacts after Satluj is taken off ZEE5: I challenge the darkness Actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh broke his silence after Satluj was taken down from ZEE5 in India until further notice. He shared a powerful scene from the film on Instagram with a defiant message.

All about Anshula Kapoor's wedding: Venue, date, timing and arrangements Influencer Anshula Kapoor is all set to marry longtime boyfriend Rohan Thakkar in an intimate Mumbai ceremony. Here's everything we know so far about the wedding date, venue and celebrations.

Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj taken down from ZEE5 India until further notice Actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj, originally titled Punjab 95, has been taken down from ZEE5 India until further notice. The streaming platform said it is exploring legal options to bring the film back and reaffirmed its support for the makers and their creative vision.

Harbhajan Singh calls Diljit's Satluj a must-watch: Truth cannot remain buried Former cricketer and MP Harbhajan Singh has praised Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj after watching the film. He appreciated the actor-singer and director Honey Trehan for telling Jaswant Singh Khalra's story.

Sohail Khan recalls father Salim's semi-coma scare: Doctor said you might lose him On Alliance, Sohail Khan recalled the time his father Salim Khan slipped into a semi-coma during hospitalisation. He said the episode deepened his fear of losing his parents and changed how he spends time with them.

Anshula Kapoor drops kalire on Orry, Janhvi, Khushi at chooda ceremony. See pics Anshula Kapoor shared heartwarming pictures from her chooda ceremony ahead of her wedding. The photos feature Arjun Kapoor, Boney Kapoor, Janhvi Kapoor, Khushi Kapoor, Shanaya Kapoor and Orry.

Watch: Aamir Khan kisses Gauri, dances with wife after she calls him her protector Videos from actor Aamir Khan and his wife Gauri Spratt's wedding show the couple exchanging vows, dancing and sharing an emotional kiss. One clip also captures Gauri calling Aamir her protector before they officially accepted each other as husband and wife.

GDN trailer: R Madhavan brings legendary inventor GD Naidu's story to life The makers of GDN have released the official trailer of the R Madhavan-starrer, offering a glimpse into the inspiring life and struggles of legendary Indian inventor G.D. Naidu. The biographical period drama is directed by Krishnakumar Ramakumar.

Lock Upp 2: Shilpa Shinde joins Farah Khan's show as first wildcard contestant Shilpa Shinde has entered Lock Upp Season 2 as its first wildcard contestant. Her entry comes shortly after she made headlines over her admission regarding the 2017 sexual harassment complaint against producer Sanjay Kohli.

Aamir greets media in 1st public appearance after wedding, wife Gauri Spratt skips Aamir Khan greeted the media after marrying Gauri Spratt at his home. The appearance followed the release of the first official wedding photograph from their private ceremony.

Alliance: Niti Taylor reveals ex-boyfriend burnt her with cigarette, slapped her On Alliance, Niti Taylor said an ex-boyfriend burnt her with a cigarette and slapped her. Her account drew focus to surviving abuse and the vulnerability she felt when she was younger.

Lock Upp 2: Madhuri Grover faces backlash over 'rich should have more kids' remark On Lock Upp 2, Madhuri Jain Grover spoke about wanting a third child and made a remark linking childbirth to wealth and poverty. The comment drew sharp criticism online, overshadowing the episode's eviction and Kangana Ranaut's guest appearance.

First photo: Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt sign marriage papers surrounded by family Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt married at their Bandra home on Sunday morning. The first image from the ceremony shows the couple surrounded by family as they signed the register.

Big B's Jalsa hit by Mumbai rains, flooding outside bungalow goes viral Amitabh Bachchan's iconic bungalow, Jalsa, was surrounded by floodwater as heavy monsoon rains lashed Mumbai, with videos from outside the residence going viral online. It remains unclear whether the actor's customary Sunday darshan will be affected by the waterlogging.

Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt are now married Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt got married in a private ceremony at his Bandra home. Their wedding followed a 25-year association and a relationship that began two years ago.

Samay Raina books entire theatre for Alia-Sharvari's Alpha, invites 250 fans Samay Raina has booked an entire Mumbai theatre for an Alpha screening after Alia Bhatt and Sharvari appeared on India's Got Latent. The gesture comes as the film draws mixed reviews, early box office chatter and support from Karan Johar.

Is Naseeruddin Shah right about success of hypermasculine films? Box office verdict A viral clip has revived Naseeruddin Shah's critique of hyper-masculine Indian cinema. Box office figures from 2024 to mid-2026 show alpha heroes still dominate, though softer films have also found audiences.

Alpha box office Day 2: Alia Bhatt's film inches closer to Rs 40 crore worldwide Alia Bhatt's Alpha has inched closer to the Rs 40-crore mark worldwide after registering a modest jump in collections on its first Saturday. The YRF Spy Universe film has now grossed Rs 37.90 crore globally in two days.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan: How delay before release has added to the hype Vijay's Jana Nayagan has kept returning to the spotlight over the months despite getting stuck with censor woes. The delay has only added an element of intrigue.

general news.headline: Mamata claims 'house arrest' over bid to meet Baruipur victim's familyIn a post on X, the party alleged that the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP)-led administration in West Bengal, after learning of Banerjee's proposed visit, deployed a large police force and erected barricades outside her residence to stop her from leaving for Baruipur.

War made us stronger: UK-based Iranian woman travels 4,000 km to mourn KhameneiAs an unending stream of people continues to pour into the Grand Mosalla, Tehran presents a striking picture: a capital in mourning, a nation still carrying the wounds of war, and a leadership that, at least for now, appears to have succeeded in rallying much of Iran behind it.

Diljit Dosanjh reacts after Satluj is taken off ZEE5: I challenge the darknessActor-singer Diljit Dosanjh broke his silence after Satluj was taken down from ZEE5 in India until further notice. He shared a powerful scene from the film on Instagram with a defiant message.

BRA 1-2 NOR Live: Neymar scores as Brazil hope for last-gasp equaliserBrazil (BRA) vs Norway (NOR) Live, FIFA World Cup: (BRA 1-2 NOR) Neymar converts from the penalty spot to give Brazil a late lifeline in stoppage time. The forward calmly beats Orjan Nyland to make it 2-1, but the Selecao have only seconds remaining to find a dramatic equaliser and force extra time.

Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt are now marriedAamir Khan and Gauri Spratt got married in a private ceremony at his Bandra home. Their wedding followed a 25-year association and a relationship that began two years ago.

In first E20 protest, motorists claim drop in mileage, choked car fuel systemFrom stalled engines and falling mileage to costly repairs, allegations made on social media were heard on the streets of Delhi as people protested against the implementation of the ethanol-blending policy. Motorists at Delhi's Jantar Mantar claimed E20 petrol was damaging their cars, and that they had evidence of that.

Netanyahu's India rejoinder to JD Vance's 'US Israel's only ally' remarkBenjamin Netanyahu responded with a touch of sarcasm, saying Israel had "some other friends" besides the White House. "We have some other friends, like a small country called India. It has 1.4 billion people, and boy, do we have tremendous support there," he said.

Aryna Sabalenka crashes out as Naomi Osaka reaches maiden Wimbledon quartersNaomi Osaka defeated World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka 6-2, 7-6(2) in a statement Centre Court display on Sunday to reach her maiden Wimbledon quarter-final.

The Swades moment: Quiet Ghar Wapsi of India's best facultyFor decades, India's brightest minds left home to shape the world's universities, laboratories and boardrooms. Today, many are returning, not out of nostalgia, but because India's biggest ideas can now be built at home. The country's quiet academic ghar wapsi may well be its most consequential Swadeshi movement yet.

6 killed after portion of chawl collapses in Mumbai's Mankhurd amid rainsAt least six people were killed and one was injured after a portion of a ground-plus-three-storey chawl collapsed in Mumbai's Mankhurd on Sunday night amid heavy rains.

Great injustice reversed: Trump on FIFA's shocking reversal of US striker's banFIFA World Cup 2026: US President Donald Trump welcomed FIFA's decision to suspend the implementation of Folarin Balogun's match ban, clearing the USA striker to face Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16.

Jannik Sinner scripts another slice of history, sweeps into Wimbledon quartersDefending champion Jannik Sinner stormed into his fifth consecutive Wimbledon quarterfinal with a dominant straight-sets win over Shintaro Mochizuki, extending his winning streak at the All England Club to 11.

Collapsed lung threatened his JEE dream, but mother's notes helped him reach IIT DelhiWhen Gunjan Kumar was bedridden with pneumothorax before JEE Advanced, his mother attended online classes and made notes for him. Her quiet intervention, alongside his resolve and teachers' guidance, helped the Kota student overcome illness and severe vision loss to move towards IIT Delhi.

12-year-old girl found dead in Bengal, man lynched over rape-murder suspicionA man accused of raping and murdering a 12-year-old girl in South 24 Parganas died after he was allegedly lynched by the victim's family and local residents. The girl's body was recovered from a pond after locals caught one accused, sparking protests and a heavy police deployment.

Maharashtra monsoon: Tree falls kill 3, injure 2 in Mumbai; Pune on red alertMumbai and Pune shut schools after IMD warnings of heavy rain across Maharashtra. The alert has deepened disruption, with deaths, waterlogging and emergency teams on standby.

POK boils over: 1 killed, many injured after Pak Rangers open fire on protestersFresh protests in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir turned violent after police allegedly opened fire on demonstrators, killing one person. The clashes came amid an expanding government crackdown and the reported arrests of more than 600 civil rights activists.

Satellite captures twin monster systems drenching Maharashtra, West BengalISRO satellite images showed two powerful monsoon systems driving intense rain over Maharashtra and West Bengal. The twin systems have strengthened the southwest monsoon but also raised flood and disruption risks.

Rs 5-crore gold-plated Ramcharitmanas moved from Ram Temple: Ex-Union Home SecyFormer Union Home Secretary S Lakshminarayanan has alleged that a gold-plated copy of the Ramcharitmanas worth around Rs 5 crore, donated by his family, was moved from the Ram Temple, with the Trust saying not every offering can be displayed.

Ruthless Australia blow away England, clinch 7th Women's T20 World Cup titleAustralia produced a ruthless, dominant performance to crush England and win a record-extending seventh Women's T20 World Cup title with a seven-wicket victory at Lord's Cricket Ground.

Mumbai schools, colleges shut tomorrow as IMD issues orange alert for heavy rainThe BMC has declared a holiday for all schools and colleges in Mumbai on July 6 after the IMD issued an Orange Alert for heavy rainfall and strong winds. Government and private offices will function as usual amid the adverse weather forecast.

How Mysore's Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan helped America gain IndependenceAs the US marks 250 years since its Declaration of Independence, here's why Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan, the rulers of India's Mysore, were admired in America during its war of independence against Britain. This is how the war in Mysore stretched British power, which played a decisive role in the American War of Independence.

Married 72 days ago, woman falls to death in Delhi; family alleges murderAkriti Sutar, a 28-year-old newlywed, died after an alleged fall from a Lodhi Colony building on Saturday evening. Her family has accused her husband and in-laws of murder as police begin inquest proceedings.

Mehbooba Mufti, in Tehran for Khamenei's funeral, says his death motivated IranMehbooba Mufti also revealed that she has written to Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of the late Supreme Leader, to convey her condolences following his father's death in the US-Israeli airstrike on February 28.

Man finds rain-soaked diary on roadside, every page tells a heartwarming storyA man found a rain-soaked diary found on a roadside and appealed for help tracing its owner. The handwritten keepsake stirred viewers, who hoped the damaged memories could still be recovered.

sports.headline: Jannik Sinner scripts another slice of history, sweeps into Wimbledon quarters Defending champion Jannik Sinner stormed into his fifth consecutive Wimbledon quarterfinal with a dominant straight-sets win over Shintaro Mochizuki, extending his winning streak at the All England Club to 11.

Cristiano Ronaldo confirms he's playing his last World Cup Cristiano Ronaldo confirmed the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be the last of his illustrious career, saying he wants to savour every moment of the tournament. The Portugal captain also expressed hope that his side's campaign against Spain will not be his final match.

Sabalenka vents frustration after Wimbledon exit: Want to get completely drunk Aryna Sabalenka admitted she wanted to get completely drunk after her Wimbledon title hopes ended with a straight-sets defeat to Naomi Osaka, who outplayed the World No. 1 to reach her maiden quarter-final at the All England Club.

BRA 0-1 NOR Live: Haaland's brace puts Norway on the brink of historic win Brazil (BRA) vs Norway (NOR) Live, FIFA World Cup: (BRA 0-2 NOR) Erling Haaland scores his second goal of the night to all but send Norway into the World Cup quarter-finals. The striker takes his tournament tally to seven as Brazil's hopes evaporate deep into stoppage time after a clinical finish.

Aryna Sabalenka crashes out as Naomi Osaka reaches maiden Wimbledon quarters Naomi Osaka defeated World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka 6-2, 7-6(2) in a statement Centre Court display on Sunday to reach her maiden Wimbledon quarter-final.

Ruthless Australia blow away England, clinch 7th Women's T20 World Cup title Australia produced a ruthless, dominant performance to crush England and win a record-extending seventh Women's T20 World Cup title with a seven-wicket victory at Lord's Cricket Ground.

Great injustice reversed: Trump on FIFA's shocking reversal of US striker's ban FIFA World Cup 2026: US President Donald Trump welcomed FIFA's decision to suspend the implementation of Folarin Balogun's match ban, clearing the USA striker to face Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16.

Novak Djokovic breaks Roger Federer's Wimbledon record, reaches quarter-finals Novak Djokovic battled past Roman Safiullin to reach the Wimbledon 2026 quarter-finals, registering a record 106th men's singles win at SW19 to surpass Roger Federer. Jessica Pegula and Karolina Muchova also advanced to the last eight.

Watch: Nat Sciver-Brunt, baby Theo create unforgettable T20 World Cup final moment England captain Nat Sciver-Brunt melted hearts by walking onto the Lord's field with her infant son Theo before the Women's T20 World Cup final, showing the growing acceptance of motherhood in elite cricket.

Lewis Hamilton takes British GP podium in P3 after late drama, Leclerc wins Formula 1: Lewis Hamilton finished third for Ferrari at the British Grand Prix in a race defined by late safety car drama and strategic calls that reshaped the podium. Charles Leclerc won the race, while George Russell took second as late incidents, including Max Verstappen's crash, neutralised the final stages.

Why Cabo Verde's 51st Independence day felt extra special this year FIFA World Cup 2026: Cabo Verde returned home to packed streets and a thunderous airport welcome after their World Cup exit. The reception on the nation's 51st Independence Day showed how this run had become a marker of national pride.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for 2027 World Cup? Ex-spinner makes bold ODI predictionL Sivaramakrishnan has urged India to consider Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for the 2027 World Cup. The former spinner said the teenager should be backed in white-ball cricket as a long-term match-winner.

Day 7 Highlights: Sinner beats Mochizuki, advances to quarterfinalsWimbledon Day 7 Live Score: Jannik Sinner beat Japan's Shintaro Mochizuki and advanced to the quarterfinals. Earlier, Novak Djokovic beat Roman Safiullin on Centre Court and advanced to the quarters. Aryna Sabalenka has been knocked out after losing to Naomi Osaka.

Not Cape Verde hero, Real Madrid sign Dutch star in right-back positionReal Madrid have completed the signing of Netherlands international Denzel Dumfries from Inter Milan, ending speculation over their right-back search. This brings a momentary end to Sidny Lopez Cabral dream of playing under Jose Mourinho for the second time in his career.

No pills, no peace? England deny Viagra claims as noisy Mexican fans ruin sleepFIFA World Cup Round of 16: Thomas Tuchel denied reports that England used Viagra before facing Mexico at the Azteca. The odd exchange came as noisy Mexican fans outside the team hotel added to England's challenge of playing at a higher altitude.

Gurnoor impresses with 10-wicket haul, Sudharsan with twin hundreds vs SL AFast bowler Gurnoor Brar ripped through Sri Lanka A in the second and final unofficial Test of the tour. India No.3, Sai Sudharsan, playing as an opener in the series, scored twin centuries as well.

Sanju Samson dropped, are you kidding me? Manjrekar stirs the pot after T20I snubSanjay Manjrekar questioned India's call to drop Sanju Samson for the second T20I against England despite handing Vaibhav Sooryavanshi a record debut. The decision and India's defeat sharpened scrutiny on team balance, considering the abundance of left-handers in the batting unit.

FIFA World Cup: Brazil, England sweat over injuries ahead of Round of 16 gamesBrazil and England are sweating over key injury concerns ahead of their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clashes. Both Carlo Ancelotti and Thomas Tuchel are readying for tough challenges.

Ghost banished: Grigor Dimitrov rewrites Wimbledon script to beat BerrettiniGrigor Dimitrov beat Matteo Berrettini in five sets to reach the Wimbledon fourth round. The win completed his comeback from last year's tearful injury exit and revived his hopes of reaching a Slam quarterfinal for the first time in two years.

Brazil play Norway in Round of 16. Will strangest World Cup curse strike again?Brazil are playing Norway in their Round of 16 clash in the FIFA World Cup. The 5-time champions will face a steep test against Erling Haaland and Martin Odegaard's side.

Shan Masood sacked, Babar Azam back: Pakistan's cricket musical chair on againBabar Azam returns as Pakistan's Test captain for the upcoming tours of the West Indies and England, despite a prolonged lean patch. He replaces the sacked Shan Masood, who paid the price for a dismal run of 12 defeats in 16 Tests.

India's mini-slump in T20Is: What's gone wrong with Shreyas Iyer's team?India slumped to their third loss in four matches under the captaincy of Shreyas Iyer. After their defeat in Manchester, Ishan Kishan said that it was important for the team to band together right now.

Like McEnroe: Kapil Dev's candid take on Virat Kohli's aggression, Test retirementKapil Dev reveals his disappointment over Virat Kohli's Test retirement, suggesting it was fuelled by anger. The 1983 World Cup-winning captain also compared the star batsman's fiery on-field aggression to tennis icon John McEnroe's legendary combative genius.

How the mighty have fallen: What went wrong for Germany and ItalyOnce football's gold standard, Germany and Italy are no longer setting the benchmark. Germany lost its edge. Italy lost its way. Both have watched the rest of the football world overtake them. The climb back to the top will demand far more than another man in the dugout.

England Women are flying, but can they break the Australia hoodoo in final?Women's T20 World Cup, AUSW vs ENGW: Nat Sciver-Brunt's England and Sophie Molineux's Australia will lock horns in the blockbuster final on Sunday, July 5, at the iconic Lord's Cricket Ground.

Mbappe refuses to shake hands, agitated Gill throws ball at France strikerFrance reached the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals after Kylian Mbappe's penalty sealed a 1-0 win over Paraguay, but the match ended with a bizarre confrontation as goalkeeper Orlando Gill threw the ball at the French captain.

Gambhir consoles Samson in long chat after dropping star from T20I teamIND vs ENG: India head coach Gautam Gambhir was seen having a long chat with star opener Sanju Samson during India's 2nd T20I against England. The team management decided to rest Samson after three straight failures and give his spot to the prodigiously talented Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

Kylian Mbappe: If we have to get our hands dirty, we'll do itFIFA World Cup 2026: France's Kylian Mbappe said that his side are willing to fight dirty if it means victory following their scrappy win against Paraguay in the Round of 16.

Mbappe settles battle of wills as France edge resilient Paraguay into quartersFIFA World Cup 2026: Kylian Mbappe's second-half penalty finally broke Paraguay's stubborn resistance as France secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory in sweltering conditions to book a World Cup quarter-final against Morocco after an attritional last-16 contest.

PAR 0-1 FRA Highlights: Mbappe's spot kick seals win for France in PhiladelphiaParaguay vs France Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (PAR 0-1 FRA) Mbappe scores from the spot to give France the lead in Philadelphia in a game which had remained deadlocked until the foul on Doue in the box.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
